fr:title.text: Type judgement in bidirectional type systems

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Type judgement in bidirectional type systems

fr:mainmatter.fr:p:
Bidirectional type systems divide the type judgement e:A into two kinds:
e \Leftarrow A, or check
e \Rightarrow A, or synthesize

This division allows type checking to be split into two modes or operations, namely, checking and synthesis. Checking and synthesis can be thought of determining the direction of flow of type information.
In checking, the type information flows from the expected type "inward" into a term. Checking enables support for features for which synthesis is too complex or even undecidable.
In synthesis, the type information flows "outward" from term. Synthesis can reduce the burden of explicit type annotations. Bidrectional type systems alternate between checking and synthesis, and the two modes can call each other.
